title: Clean-Air And Traffic Window Features
summary: Public F1 strategy analysis repeatedly highlights pit-loss, traffic gaps, and undercut range as decisive.
rationale: Public F1 strategy analysis repeatedly highlights pit-loss, traffic gaps, and undercut range as decisive. The current best code uses circuit priors but almost no explicit field-context features describing whether a stop would release the driver into clean air or dirty traffic.
implementation_hint: Within each Year-Race-LapNumber, derive position-neighbor context available on the current lap only: lap-time delta to cars +/-1 and +/-2 positions, tyre-age gap to nearby rivals, local field spread, rank of current degradation within the field, and a proxy for pit-window openness such as whether a 20-25s loss would drop the driver into a dense pack vs sparse zone. Interact with Position and RaceProgress.
expected_effect: Should add orthogonal signal for undercut/overcut viability and explain track-specific stop timing better than static race priors alone.
risk: True time gaps are absent, so proxies may be noisy. Over-engineering field features can hurt if CV does not preserve full race groups.
